page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-64-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs and 248.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 3.88 GB per vCPU. The instance configuration includes six local solid-state drives as indicated by its basic specification, though it does not include any integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its dedicated CPU allocation, this server provides predictable compute performance for multi-threaded workloads. The balance of 64 vCPUs and 248.0 GB of RAM aligns closely with standard general-purpose resource ratios, offering a stable environment for applications requiring high core counts without extreme memory footprints. A notable tradeoff is the absence of hardware acceleration, making it unsuitable for GPU-dependent tasks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 64 dedicated cores combined with local SSD storage provides a consolidated platform that reduces the need for multiple smaller instances.

The c4d-standard-64-lssd is designed for workloads that benefit from high-core parallel processing and local storage performance. Suitable use cases include database hosting, web serving at scale, continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require dedicated compute resources and fast local disk access.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-64-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server within the c4d family, configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 248.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers a balanced resource profile of approximately 3.88 GB of memory per core. The server includes six local SSDs for high-speed local storage, but lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the instance provides predictable performance for multi-threaded applications. Qualitatively, the high core density and bundled local storage offer a cost-efficient consolidation path for workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for database hosting, large-scale web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring robust parallel processing.

tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core x86_64 server with two hundred forty-eight gigabytes of RAM and six local solid-state drives for balanced enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for predictable parallel processing
248.0 GB of system memory providing 3.88 GB per core
Equipped with 6 local SSDs for high-speed local storage
No integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Optimized for database hosting, CI/CD pipelines, and web serving
